reasons: Angela can attach to Joy and provide protection while Joy dives through enemy formations.
Joy's high mobility gives Angela an aggressive host that can carry support effects into the backline.

proof.summary: Angela shields and supports Joy during her dash and ultimate window, helping Joy survive aggressive backline pressure.
worksBestWhen: Angela attaches before Joy fully commits.
Joy has a clear dash path and rhythm timing.
Joy has valid targets to continue her sequence.
failureCases: Joy fails to continue her dash chain.
Angela attaches after Joy has already taken lethal damage.
Enemy suppression or burst stops Joy before her ultimate value.

reasons: Floryn's global healing can support Joy even when Joy dives far from the backline.
Joy's extended movement-based fight pattern benefits from healing during the middle of the dive.

proof.summary: Floryn helps Joy survive long enough to complete her dash rhythm and ultimate damage window.
worksBestWhen: Floryn times Bloom after Joy is committed and taking damage.
Joy enters with a clear route through enemy heroes.
Joy has enough rhythm timing to activate full value.
failureCases: Joy is burst before Bloom matters.
Joy misses dash timing and cannot activate full value.

reasons: Mathilda can help Joy enter or reset position with mobility support.
Mathilda's shield helps Joy survive before she completes her rhythm-based damage window.

proof.summary: Mathilda improves Joy's entry and survivability before Joy starts her main dash sequence.
worksBestWhen: Mathilda does not force Joy into a bad angle.
Joy uses the mobility support to reach valid dash targets.
Joy keeps enough cooldown timing to continue her sequence.
failureCases: Joy loses rhythm after taking the dash.
Mathilda's support expires before Joy commits.
Enemy control catches Joy before she can continue.

reasons: Atlas can group enemies, giving Joy more targets to dash through and damage during her ultimate window.
Joy can follow Atlas's engage after enemies are pulled into a predictable area.

proof.summary: Atlas creates a grouped teamfight, while Joy uses the clustered enemies to continue her dash and AoE damage pattern.
worksBestWhen: Atlas engages after Joy is close enough to enter.
Joy waits for the grouped window before committing ultimate pressure.
Joy has a safe path through the grouped enemies.
failureCases: Atlas engages while Joy is too far away.
Enemies disengage after Atlas's control ends.
Joy dashes into crowd control before her damage window.

reasons: Chip's portals can bring Joy or her team into a fight quickly, which helps Joy join cross-map skirmishes.
Joy benefits from sudden team arrival because enemies have less time to spread out before she starts her dash pattern.

proof.summary: Chip enables fast fight access through portals, giving Joy a faster route into skirmishes where she can dash through enemies.
worksBestWhen: The portal target is a real winning fight.
Joy enters after teammates arrive, not alone into five enemies.
Joy has valid dash targets after arriving.
failureCases: The portal delivers Joy into heavy crowd control.
Joy has no valid targets to continue her dash chain.
Teammates do not use the portal together.
